title: 10 best herbal essential oils for runny nose
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for runny nose are natural remedies derived from plants that effectively alleviate congestion, itching, and discomfort associated with a runny nose. These oils work by opening up airways, reducing inflammation, and moisturizing the nasal passages. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with runny nose include eucalyptus, peppermint, and lavender. When used through steam inhalation or diffusers, these oils provide instant relief, allowing individuals to breathe easier and sleep better. This improves lives by reducing frustration, fatigue, and discomfort, ultimately promoting a sense of well-being and relaxation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties, derived from the leaves of the Eucalyptus globulus tree. The oil's potent compounds, such as eucalyptol and globulol, work to reduce nasal congestion by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel, providing swift relief from stuffiness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated nasal passages, further reducing discomfort and congestion associated with a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum oil's antibacterial properties can help combat infections that may be causing excessive mucus production and congestion in the nasal passages.
Decongestant properties: The oil's decongestant properties can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal membranes, thereby relieving sinus pressure and runny nose symptoms.
Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a soothing essential oil that helps to calm and reduce nasal congestion.
Buds: The delicate buds contain potent volatile oils that help to relieve sinus pressure and ease the symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus in early morning dew for maximum oil content retention.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with cold running water to remove impurities and dirt thoroughly.
Steam distill the washed leaves at 100°c for 2 hours to release essential oils and flavor compounds completely.
Filter the resulting oil mixture using a paper filter or coffee filter to separate oils from solids carefully.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass containers and store in cool dry place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains thymol, a compound that has been shown to reduce mucus production and ease congestion in the nasal passages. When used topically, thyme essential oil can help to thin out excess mucus, making it easier to expel and providing quick relief from stuffiness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, further alleviating sinus pressure and promoting a sense of clear airways.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections in the nasal passages, reducing congestion and runniness.

Decongestant Properties: The decongestant properties of thyme essential oil help shrink swollen blood vessels and reduce excess mucus production in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that can help to decongest the nasal passages and ease respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn when their aromatic compounds are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or air-dry them slowly.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a glass steam still and collect the essential oil fraction after 4-6 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered thymus vulgaris essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, it can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and discomfort. Additionally, tea tree oil has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat infections that may be contributing to a runny nose. By promoting healthy sinus function and fighting off infection-causing bacteria and viruses, tea tree essential oils can provide effective natural relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate a runny nose.
Decongestant properties: The oil's decongestant properties can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal tissues, providing relief from a stuffy or runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea Tree Essential Oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with allergies, colds, or sinusitis, which can contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and rinse them gently under cool running water.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7-10 days or until they become brittle.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the crushed leaf powder with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil and stir well.
Store the mixture in an amber glass bottle and let it steep for 2-3 weeks before straining and decanting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing mucus to flow more freely and alleviating congestion. Additionally, rosemary oil's anti-inflammatory effects help to soothe irritated tissues, reducing redness and discomfort associated with a runny nose. By promoting healthy sinus drainage and reducing inflammation, rosemary essential oils can provide fast relief from colds and allergies-related congestion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and protect the nasal passages from oxidative damage, which can exacerbate a runny nose.

Decongestant: Rosemary essential oil has natural decongestant properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving circulation to the affected areas.
Expectorant: Rosemary essential oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing the sensation of a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots are effective in reducing nasal congestion and relieving sinus pressure.
Leaves: As the primary source of essential oil in rosemary, leaves contain camphor and borneol, which help to decongest and soothe a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from healthy plants.
Steam distill the rosmarinus officinalis leaves using a steam distillation apparatus for 3-4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the water mixture by decantation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for 6-12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to relax and shrink swollen blood vessels in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. This allows for easier breathing and a clearer flow of air through the nose, providing relief from runny noses caused by colds, allergies, or sinus pressure. By using English lavender essential oils, you can find comfort and ease during times of respiratory distress.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine: English lavender essential oil has antihistamine properties, which helps to reduce congestion and relieve runny noses by blocking histamine receptors in the body.

Anti-inflammatory: Lavender essential oil exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can help alleviate a runny nose caused by allergic reactions or sinus infections.
Expectorant: English lavender essential oil has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion and soothing a runny nose.
Flowers: Contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, helping to ease congestion and calm the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days or until brittle and crumbly.
Steam distill the dried lavender flowers using a copper still and 1 liter of water for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil from the condenser and transfer it to glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store the lavender essential oil in a cool, dark place for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The oil's decongestant properties also work to constrict blood vessels and reduce excess mucus production, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help to combat infections that can cause a runny nose, promoting overall respiratory health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Sage essential oil helps reduce nasal congestion by constricting blood vessels, thereby decreasing swelling and reducing mucus production.
Antiviral: Sage essential oil exhibits antiviral properties that can help combat viral infections such as the common cold, which often cause runny nose, by inhibiting viral replication.
Anti-inflammatory: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used to create essential oils that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure.
Leaves: The leaves of the sage plant contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or local nursery measuring about 10g for every 100ml of carrier oil.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to preserve their medicinal properties and aroma.
Steam distill the dried salvia officinalis leaves using a steam distillation apparatus with 500ml of water for 30 minutes to produce essential oil.
Combine the distilled essential oil with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:10 and let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in an airtight container away from direct sunlight for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and thymol, work to shrink swollen nasal passages and reduce excess mucus production, providing quick relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, the antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of wild marjoram essential oils help to combat infections and soothe irritated tissues, making it an effective natural remedy for a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Expectorant Properties: Wild Marjoram essential oil helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to a runny nose.

Antimicrobial Activity: Wild Marjoram essential oil has antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections and reduce the risk of secondary bacterial infections that can exacerbate a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of wild marjoram contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making them effective for alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried origanum vulgare leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested herbs using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Crush the dried herbs into small pieces to increase their surface area for efficient steam distillation.
Combine 100g of crushed origanum vulgare with 500ml of water in a stainless steel still and heat it slowly to 100°c over 2 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil through fractional distillation, filtering out impurities to produce a high-quality therapeutic-grade oil for sinus relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The oil contains compounds like curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby helping to alleviate congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, curry plant essential oils have expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus, providing fast relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Curry plant essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, leading to a decrease in mucus production and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of curry plant essential oil can help reduce swelling and redness in the nasal tissues, providing relief from discomfort and pressure associated with a runny nose.

Expectorant properties: Curry plant essential oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the nasal passages, providing temporary relief from congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce nasal congestion and inflammation.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and relieving sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality helichrysum italicum flowers at dawn when they are most potent for future extraction.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers to prevent contamination or spoilage during processing.
Steam-distill 100g of dried helichrysum italicum flowers with 500ml of water in a copper alembic still for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and ease congestion. The menthol content in peppermint oil also has a cooling effect on the mucous membranes, helping to thin out excess mucus and promote easier drainage. This can provide fast and effective relief from sinus pressure and runny nose, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and allergies.
remedy_properties: Decongestant: Peppermint oil's decongestant properties help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure by thinning out mucus, making it easier to expel.
Expectorant: The expectorant property of peppermint oil helps loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving coughs and congestion associated with a runny nose.
Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to a runny nose and other allergy-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, which helps to ease congestion and reduce nasal secretions.
Leaves: Contain high amounts of menthone, a compound that helps to thin mucus and relieve nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market for distillation.
Wash the harvested peppermint leaves thoroughly in cold running water and pat dry gently.
Combine one part dried peppermint leaves with four parts water in a large glass jar and steep for 30 minutes.
Distill the herbal mixture using a steam distiller at a temperature of 100°c for 1 hour to obtain essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities before bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense essential oils helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. When used aromatically or topically, frankincense oil can help to clear out excess mucus and congestion, promoting healthy drainage and relief from stuffy noses. Additionally, frankincense oil's antiviral and antibacterial properties may help to combat underlying infections that can cause runny nose, making it a natural remedy for sinus pressure and congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, which can contribute to a runny nose.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of frankincense oil can help combat underlying infections or allergies that may be causing the runny nose, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Expectorant: Frankincense oil's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion and alleviating sinus pressure.
Barks: Contain potent antimicrobial agents that help combat infections causing runny noses, while also providing soothing relief from irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata leaves from a trusted supplier or cultivate them in your garden.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 70°f for 8 hours to preserve their potency.
Steam distill the dried boswellia serrata leaves using a 10% plant-to-water ratio for 4 hours and collect the essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered herbal boswellia serrata essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat runny nose with herbal essential oils is by blending eucalyptus oil, peppermint oil, and menthol oil. Add a few drops of this blend to your diffuser or inhale it directly from a cloth or tissue. Eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties help reduce nasal congestion, while peppermint oil's cooling effect soothes the nasal passages.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal essential oils are sinusitis, bronchitis, and allergies. These conditions often cause congestion, coughing, and sneezing. Herbal essential oils such as eucalyptus, peppermint, and lavender can help relieve these symptoms by opening up airways, reducing inflammation, and soothing the respiratory tract. Additionally, oils like thyme and oregano have antiviral properties that may help combat underlying infections.
